D11 ComputationalL3 ApplyReference

Octree Quantization — Tree-Structured Palette

Insert each pixel into an 8-child tree by bit-plane; merge leaves bottom-up until at most k leaves remain; O(N + log k) and streamable

Gervautz-Purgathofer (1988) RGB octree: each level tests one bit of R, G, B → 8 children. After inserting all pixels, repeatedly merge the least-populated leaf into its parent until ≤ k leaves. Streamable: works on video frames in real time, used in early Java AWT and some DNG tools.

Slide bit-depth (= tree depth). Shallow = blocky, deep = closer to truecolor.
